what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard document that serves as a framing entry point for a multi-perspective map, listing six related board items in a reading order and stating that divergent viewpoints are preserved rather than merged.
observations: YAML frontmatter includes id, address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, title, author_session, channel, order list, timestamps, and history transitions.
The body is a single short paragraph describing a map across 7 perspectives: independent convergences, real clashes, unique observations, and distilled decisions.
It explicitly says 'NOT a merge — the diversity is preserved'.
It instructs readers to 'read the 7 in the switcher'.
The order field contains six board:// item addresses.
standouts: The explicit anti-merge framing and commitment to preserving diversity.
The categorization of perspectives into signal, forks, only-one-saw, and distilled decisions.
It functions as an ordered index to other items rather than a self-contained map.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and Markdown body.
Uses board:// URI addresses for linked items.
Includes history tracking with from/to states and actor/timestamp.
concerns: The body is vague without the linked items; it relies on an external 'switcher' that is not defined here.
The title and body refer to 7 perspectives, but the order field lists only 6 item addresses, leaving the count unexplained.
notes: A draft perspective-map index that points to six related noticeboard items and frames them as preserved divergent viewpoints rather than a merged consensus.
questions: Which specific items represent the seven perspectives?
What is the 'switcher' and how does a reader access it?
What are the concrete convergences, clashes, unique observations, and distilled decisions?
Why does the order list contain six addresses if the map spans seven perspectives?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a multi-perspective map across seven viewpoints; the actual file is a short framing paragraph plus an ordered list of six linked items, so it behaves more like an entry-point index than the full map.
apparent_themes: Preservation of disagreement and diversity over premature consensus.
Lightweight navigational indexing of related documents.
intention_signals: It appears designed to be a navigational hub that frames a set of perspective documents as a structured comparison, signaling that both agreements and conflicts should be inspected together rather than collapsed into a single answer.
novelty: The explicit 'not a merge' stance, the 'switcher' metaphor for reading multiple perspectives, and the four-way categorization of convergences, clashes, unique observations, and distilled decisions.
contribution: It contributes a framing label ('Tensions & convergences') and an ordered reading list that preserves the relationship between multiple perspective items as a set, signaling that they should be interpreted together rather than reduced to a single answer.
summary_for_embedding: A draft noticeboard item titled 'Tensions & convergences — the perspective map' that frames six linked items as a seven-perspective map preserving independent convergences, real clashes, unique observations, and distilled decisions without merging them.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a navigational entry point that presents a collection of perspective documents as a deliberately non-merged map of agreements, disagreements, and decisions.
